channel.title: Food Science on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Food Science on MinWeb

item.title: The Truth About Ultra-Processed Foods and Your Health
item.description:
You’re walking down the grocery aisle hunting for a quick meal. You flip a box over and scan the ingredient list, which reads more like a chemistry experiment than a recipe. You’ve heard the term “ultra-processed” floating around, but it feels vague—a nebulous catch-all that doesn’t explain why your favorite snack leaves you feeling sluggish or why your weight is so hard to manage.

Honestly, it’s frustrating.

Is this just “bad food,” or is something more calculated happening behind the scenes?

item.title: The Biggest Nutrition Myths We Still Believe in 2026
item.description:
If you’ve spent any time on social media or health forums lately, you know the feeling: nutrition advice has become a frantic, contradictory mess. One week, the internet claims eggs will ruin your heart. The next, an AI-generated meal plan tells you to double your protein intake because you’re “wasting potential.” It’s exhausting.

As of January 2026, the 2025–2030 Dietary Guidelines for Americans (DGA) have finally provided some much-needed clarity. Yet, despite the updated science, many of us are still clinging to outdated “food rules” that were debunked years ago.
